posts.author: dtomic7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Chris Duncan, TKO, R4
Probably the only fight I will watch on this card to be honest, maybe the Ewing fight if I have the time to spare. Duncan just pours it on him in the 4th gets the finish because Moicano is not very good anymore.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Virna Jandiroba, DEC
Co-main of fight night btw, just wanna highlight the product the UFC has been putting out in recent years.

Yakhyaev vs. Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1
Yakhyaev has been a combined -4600 in his last 3 fights. I'll have to do some research but I genuinely think there has never been a fighter like this in UFC, insane stuff.

Estevam vs. Ewing
Ethyn Ewing, DEC
Man I did not know ball with Ewing when he last fought, but he looked so good. Estevam tends to slow down in later rounds, which very well could've been the effect of the weight cut, but there no way to tell.

Ruchała vs. Delano
José Delano, DEC
Delano is the real deal, the best propect to come of DWCS last season, by a country mile too.

Petersen vs. Pat
Guilherme Pat, TKO, R3
Pat more easy work, I hope the train does get the upset though.

Costa vs. Nicoll
Alessandro Costa, TKO, R2
Costa easy work.

Vannata vs. Flowers
Lando Vannata, DEC
What is this 2018???

Cowan vs. Pereira
Alice Pereira, DEC
I'm gonna take Pereira on this one just because she looks like a better prospect with much more promise, I highly doubt she wins this easy though.

Bekoev vs. Gore
Azamat Bekoev, TKO, R2
This card is probably gonna start very, very slow (although these types of fight nights always seem oi be insane) but for predictions sake Bekoev takes this one.

Barbosa vs. Gatto
Dione Barbosa, DEC
I mean its a fight.

Hope vs. Kamaka III
Dakota Hope, DEC
Hope is the better fighter everywhere from the tape I've seen on both guys. Decision seems good here.

posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Chris Duncan, TKO, R2
I think this is gonna come down to Duncan’s grit. He’s not gonna respect Moicano’s striking. He kind of respects Moicano’s grappling because they're training partners and he said he could never sub Moicano. And this is a huge fight for both of them. I doubt Duncan goes for a stupid sub attempt that he knows would get him in trouble and keep it on the feet for the most part. And out-brawl a weaker 37 year old Moicano. Duncan does real damage Moicano doesn’t. The BSD performance will never happen again. It was perfect and still he didn’t put him out and BSD fought a decent 2nd round. Give me Duncan to land something heavy and finish it with G&P. R.I.P Money Moicano.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Tabatha Ricci, DEC
I hate this pick. I think Jandiroba’s better, bigger, and has the masculine advantage. Even though Jandiroba was 1 round off being champ, styles make fights. Jandiroba can’t strike and Ricci is hard to control. Jandiroba would have to win by control time/sub attempts. And all Ricci has to do is not get controlled, which is her specialty, and force Jandiroba to strike, which is her weakness. And Ricci usually has the sway of the Judges. 29-28.

Yakhyaev vs. Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1
Yakhyaev is one of the best prospects in the UFC. He will get a highlight finish inside 2 minutes. Spinning wheel kick?, Flying knee?, 1-2 That puts him down?, G&P?, Sub?, Who knows.

Estevam vs. Ewing
Rafael Estevam, DEC
Ethan Ewing fans are some of the most ridiculous people I know. It was clear that outside of Wellmakers right hand he had nothing else in his toolbag. After it landed, Ewing ate it. It was wraps. Welcome to a real UFC fighter. Every fight Estevam is the worse striker, it's nothing new. I’ve seen Ewing taken down before and Estevam is good and persistent at it and a serious threat on the ground. If InnerG can get controlled and taken down by Estevam, why should a newcomer who beat a one trick pony striker? I think Ewing’s ok on the ground, smart, has good bodywork, good combos. Is the better striker obviously but lacks in the TDD against dudes not on Estevam’s level. The move up to 135 should help Estevam's cardio as well.

Ruchała vs. Delano
José Delano, DEC
My 2nd favorite prospect off DWCS. Jose is a world-beater, He has a Great stiff jab, Head movement, Footwork, Power, Cardio, Timing, Placement, Bodywork, Good defense, Good ground game, Great TDD, Good get ups. Like Lerryan he was Great in LFA, Really showed out on the Contender Series. Ruchala was cool on KSW but he’s kinda low volume and gets hit. I rate Gomis so the loss wasn’t that bad. Ruchala is a decent fighter honestly they aren’t helping him with the matchups but Jose is here to change the division. KO is also very live. Ruchala will be open to get countered, Delano is a sniper.

Petersen vs. Pat
Guilherme Pat, TKO, R3
6 '5, Athletic, Good striking, Good power, Good TDD in his debut atleast. If Petersen holds him against the cage I know Pat showed no urgency to get off last fight but he beat Frye up even in the clinch. If Petersen can’t damage him I just see him getting pieced, trying to smudge, and getting pieced until Pat KO’s him late or they both gas and Pat wins by just being able to throw strikes. Petersen beat Mayes and Usman the two lowest level guys in the division who showed close to no threat. And Petersen has a trash gas tank and isn’t dangerous himself. At least Pat can strike and stop a TD.

* Edited at 04.03.2026,  5:01 PM ET *

Barbsosa vs. Gotta feels so damn tough to call. Both girls have disposed of lower level competition and struggled with better competition. When I watch tape Barbosa seems more talented. Gatto's time off is also such a hard thing to factor in

posts.author: Fast_Tulip
posts.content:
[Predictions]

I unashamedly watched all of Pereira's regional fights and wasn't impressed in any of them. It just seemed like she weight bullied a bunch of smaller chicks into grappling exchanges. Her RNC win doesn't even look like it was fully locked in. Two of her wins are KOs in like 10 seconds which obviously isn't a real win and there's no footage of them. I thought her big looping hooks would have some power when I watched her throw them in her debut fight, but even the regional chicks ate them so not much power there. She seems taller than her height listed and a bit heavier. If Cowan showed more aggression, I would be way more confident in her. Cowan unfortunately doesn't really throw a ton of volume, wrestling isn't that great, and doesn't really BJJ. On a side note, Pereira hasn't really been uploading any training footage on her insta and seems to be mostly focused on traveling for what it's worth whereas Cowan is now training fulltime at Xtreme Couture. Cowan seems really motivated to get back in the ring and seems to enjoy her current training partners. Pereira has the same coach as Raveena Oliveira, the girl who is winless in the UFC. I think experience probably helps Cowan win it. Even though some of her opponents didn't have a ton of pro fights, at least they had a bunch of regional experience. I'm surprisingly really interested to see what happens here
